title_en: China’s rulers have a woman problem
title_zh: 中国的统治者有一个女性问题
rubric_en: Antagonism between the sexes does nothing to address China’s demographic problems
rubric_zh: 性别对立无助于解决中国的人口问题

paragraphs.en: CHINA HAS no direct translation of the word “manosphere” but it is plagued by one nonetheless—indeed, the abuse of women on social media is every bit as poisonous there as it is in democracies. The difference is that, in a country where censors rush to silence posts they dislike, the authorities tolerate the manosphere’s vitriol and instead focus their energy on women. The cyberspace regulator’s crackdown on harmful content specifically lists “extreme feminism”, including posts that promote singledom.
paragraphs.zh: 中文里没有“manosphere”（厌女男性网络圈层）这个词的直接对应译法，但中国同样深受其害。事实上，社交媒体上对女性的辱骂在那里和在民主国家一样恶毒。不同之处在于，在一个审查者会迅速压制他们不喜欢的帖子的国家，当局却容忍这种男性圈层的刻毒言论，反而把精力集中在女性身上。网信监管机构打击有害内容的行动明确列出“极端女权主义”，其中包括宣扬单身的帖子。

paragraphs.en: This is just one symptom of a much broader problem that China’s old, male rulers have with the opposite sex. Unlike Asia’s other two big powers, Japan and India, China has never been led by a woman in modern times. As China’s leader, Xi Jinping, has consolidated power, he has also sidelined women. The powerful Politburo, which usually has 24 members, has been exclusively male since 2022. No woman has ever made it to the more important Politburo Standing Committee. No wonder China has been tumbling down international rankings of gender equality.
paragraphs.zh: 这只是中国那些年迈的男性统治者在面对异性时所暴露出的一个更广泛问题的症状。不同于亚洲另外两个大国日本和印度，中国现代以来从未由女性领导。随着中国领导人习近平巩固权力，他也把女性排挤到了边缘。通常有24名成员、权力很大的政治局，自2022年以来一直清一色为男性。更重要的政治局常委会从未有女性进入。难怪中国在国际性别平等排名中一路下滑。

paragraphs.en: At the heart of Communist Party policies towards women is a crushing error. The party is worried about the country’s demography. But it imagines the solution lies in browbeating women and telling them, in traditional tones, to be “virtuous wives and good mothers”. The result is an approach to women that will not only fail to realise the party’s narrow aims, but will also make countless women miserable.
paragraphs.zh: 共产党对女性政策的核心，是一个沉重的错误。党担心国家的人口问题。但它想象中的解决办法，是用传统口吻训诫女性，要求她们做“贤妻良母”。结果，这种对待女性的方式不仅无法实现党那些狭隘目标，还会让无数女性痛苦不堪。

paragraphs.en: Mao Zedong famously claimed that women hold up “half the sky”, but you would never know it today. The party’s disastrous one-child policy, in place until 2015, led to millions of female fetuses being aborted. The inevitable result is that millions of women are now missing. Their absence leads to other terrible outcomes. Counting men aged 23-37 and women aged 22-36, China has 22.5m more men than women. To be a catch, a man needs an education, a solid job, a flat and, in many regions, to pay a hefty fee to his bride’s family. Around a third of young male migrants who intend to marry think they have only a 50% or lower chance of doing so by the age of 30.
paragraphs.zh: 毛泽东有句名言，说妇女能顶“半边天”，但今天你几乎看不出这一点。党灾难性的一孩政策一直实施到2015年，导致数以百万计的女性胎儿被流产。不可避免的结果是，如今有数以百万计的女性“缺失”。她们的缺席又导致其他可怕后果。按23至37岁的男性和22至36岁的女性计算，中国男性比女性多出2250万。一个男人若想成为抢手的结婚对象，需要有教育背景、稳定工作、一套房；在许多地区，还要向新娘家庭支付一大笔彩礼。约三分之一有结婚打算的年轻男性流动人口认为，自己到30岁时结婚的概率只有50%或更低。

paragraphs.en: As a result, resentments abound: many men, especially rural ones, are chauvinists. The sense of bitterness is likely to spread. Women make up more than half of those in higher education; men who lack education or economic prospects risk falling ever further behind.
paragraphs.zh: 结果是怨气四处弥漫：许多男性，尤其是农村男性，抱有大男子主义观念。这种苦涩感很可能继续扩散。接受高等教育的人中，女性占比超过一半；缺乏教育或经济前景的男性则有可能越来越落后。

paragraphs.en: The Chinese government cannot suddenly conjure up millions of missing women to alleviate the situation. But it could introduce policies that boost the marriage rate among Chinese women while improving the choices available to them.
paragraphs.zh: 中国政府无法突然凭空变出数以百万计缺失的女性来缓解局面。但它可以出台一些政策，在提高中国女性结婚率的同时，也改善她们可获得的选择。

paragraphs.en: One idea is to ensure that women have proper rights within marriages. The enforcement of laws on harassment, marital rape and domestic violence is woefully inadequate. More might marry if they were assured of fair treatment in cases of divorce, including payment of child support. If it were easier for wives to divorce bad husbands, more might remarry.
paragraphs.zh: 一个想法是确保女性在婚姻中拥有切实的权利。有关骚扰、婚内强奸和家庭暴力的法律执行严重不足。如果女性确信离婚时能获得公平对待，包括子女抚养费能够得到支付，或许会有更多人愿意结婚。如果妻子更容易与糟糕的丈夫离婚，或许也会有更多人再婚。

paragraphs.en: Fairer divorce settlements might also hasten the end of bride prices. Research suggests a young male migrant must save for six years to afford a bride price of 127,300 yuan ($18,780). A law bans extortionate bride prices already, but it is ambiguous and poorly enforced. Many parents still set great store by the price a daughter can command; the desperation of men to find a partner means that those with money will pay; female divorcees sometimes retain a chunk of it if marriages fail. But the payment both commodifies women and disadvantages impecunious men in rural areas in particular.
paragraphs.zh: 更公平的离婚财产安排也可能加速彩礼的终结。研究显示，一名年轻男性流动人口必须储蓄六年，才负担得起127300元人民币（18780美元）的彩礼。法律已经禁止索要过高彩礼，但规定含糊，执行不力。许多父母仍然非常看重女儿能要到多少彩礼；男性寻找伴侣的迫切感意味着有钱的人会支付；如果婚姻破裂，离异女性有时会保留其中一大部分。但这种支付既把女性商品化，也让缺钱的男性处于不利地位，尤其是在农村地区。

paragraphs.en: More generally, the government could lower the hurdles to home ownership and work that stand in the way of couples. It should buy more of China’s vast tracts of unsold housing, converting it into affordable rental homes favoured by young families. Recent relaxations in the hukou system of internal passports, which make it easier for newcomers to register themselves and their families for schools and other public services in cities, are welcome.
paragraphs.zh: 更广泛地说，政府可以降低挡在情侣面前的购房和工作门槛。它应当购买中国大面积未售出的住房，将其改造成受年轻家庭欢迎的可负担租赁住房。最近对户口这一内部通行证制度的放宽值得欢迎，因为这让新来者更容易在城市为自己和家人登记，获得学校和其他公共服务。

paragraphs.en: Traditionalist men, including those at the top of the Communist Party, may see some of these ideas as foreign impositions. If they stopped chiding Chinese women and tried asking them for their opinions, they might hear a different story. ■
paragraphs.zh: 传统主义男性，包括共产党最高层的一些人，可能会把其中一些想法视为外来的强加之物。如果他们停止斥责中国女性，转而试着询问她们的意见，或许会听到另一种说法。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订户可以订阅我们的Opinion新闻简报，其中汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。
